description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden enclosure and a black front panel. The front features a large speaker grille on the left, a horizontal frequency display with labeled bands, and a row of silver tuning and control knobs below. A power cord with a two-prong plug extends from the back, indicating it is electrically powered. The top and sides are finished in a wood veneer, while the back panel is plain with some labeling and ventilation slots. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century home audio equipment, combining both functional and decorative elements.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a black front panel, speaker grille, tuning display, and control knobs, plus a power cord extending from the back.
description_view_0: Front view showing the speaker grille, tuning display, control knobs, and brand label.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the wood veneer side panel and the power cord exiting the back.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the plain back panel with labeling, ventilation slots, and the attached power cord.
description_view_3: Right side view highlighting the wood veneer side and the power cord trailing from the back.
